name: Gravel tour from Olot through the Garrotxa natural park
name_original: Vuelta gravel desde Olot por el parque natural de la Garrotxa
description:
The nerve center of the Garrotxa, Olot is an excellent starting point for the first ring on Spanish roads. Head along the river in a north-west direction, towards Hostalnou de Bianya, where a first glimpse of woods and dirt roads will give you an idea of what awaits you. Once you have crossed the asphalt provincial road, take a fast serpentine that recalls the valleys of the Flemish hills to reach Sant Joan les Fonts. Here the church of San Giovanni Battista, a waterfall, a Roman bridge and a panoramic point over some ancient lava flows will delight you.

Continue on country roads along the Fluvià river to reach a truly surprising destination, Castellfollit de la Roca. Located on a hill of basalt rock, Castellfollit is part of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Nature Reserve, which with over 40 volcanic cones that have been extinct for centuries is considered the most significant volcanic area of the Iberian peninsula. If up to now the road has always or almost always been slightly downhill, at the exit of Besalù a climb on a mixed concrete and asphalt surface allows you to enter a path that is as lost in the woods as it is magnificent.

It is a very long track, full of ups and downs and dust: a true paradise for those who love gravel. Don't let yourself be found without water here, because you will only come across one fountain near the town of El Torn. Go up the Ser river to Santa Pau, where you can take a break before entering the actual volcano area. Just beyond the very black volcano of Rocanegra, tie your bike to some trees (it is forbidden to cycle up there) and walk up to the cone of the Santa Margherita volcano: it is not a long walk and allows you to observe the spectacular little church built inside of the extinguished cone. You can do the same thing, parking your bike at the park ranger's house, for the Croscat volcano: one of its slopes has been excavated, so you will have the impression of seeing inside the belly of the volcano itself. An incredible show.

Subsequently, a silent beech forest takes you back towards Olot in the best possible way. The first day in Garrotxa could not have been more exciting.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Olot offers diverse routes through the unique volcanic landscape of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. The region features approximately 40 volcanic cones and numerous lava flows, shaping a rolling terrain with varied soil colors. Extensive forests, including the distinctive Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest growing on a lava flow, provide shaded pathways. The area is also characterized by well-developed greenways like the Via Verde del Carrilet, which follows an old railway line. Best gravel bike trails around Olot The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel tour from Olot through the Garrotxa natural park, a 55.9 miles (89.9 km) trail that takes 7 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the heart of the volcanic natural park. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Vía Verde Olot–Girona – Col de Bas loop from Reserva natural parcial del volcà Montolivet, a difficult 30.1 miles (48.5 km) path. This route combines sections of the greenway with scenic loops through the volcanic foothills. Local gravel bikers also love the Col de Bas – El Torn Village loop from Olot, a 43.2 miles (69.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain and villages, often completed in about 5 hours 56 minutes. Gravel biking around Olot is defined by volcanic terrain, lush forests including the Fageda d'en Jordà, and accessible greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Olot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Olot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Olot?
A: Olot offers a wide selection of gravel biking opportunities, with over 25 distinct routes available. These range from moderate rides to challenging expeditions through the region's unique volcanic landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Olot?
A: The terrain around Olot is primarily shaped by its volcanic origins, featuring a rolling landscape with varied soil colors from past lava flows. You'll encounter lush forests, including the distinctive Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest growing on a lava flow, and well-developed greenways like the Via Verde del Carrilet, which offers mostly compacted gravel surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy gravel routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39553789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Olot–Girona – Col de Bas loop from Olot&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.5 km route that offers a less strenuous experience, combining sections of the greenway with scenic loops through the volcanic foothills.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced gravel bikers in Olot?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging routes. For experienced riders, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371548761/gravel-tour-from-olot-through-the-garrotxa-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel tour from Olot through the Garrotxa natural park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 89.9 km trail that explores the heart of the volcanic natural park, featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see along the gravel bike trails in Olot?
A: Many routes pass through the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, allowing you to explore unique features. You can encounter volcanic cones like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt;, known for its chapel inside the crater, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;, which reveal layers of volcanic materials. The Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest is another magical setting, especially in autumn.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in Olot?
A: Yes, many gravel routes around Olot are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22314002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Olot–Girona – Col de Bas loop from Reserva natural parcial del volcà Montolivet&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 48.5 km circular path that combines greenway sections with scenic loops.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the trails in Olot?
A: The gravel biking experience in Olot is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Riders frequently praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the diverse forests, and the accessibility of greenways like the Via Verde del Carrilet, which offer a blend of challenging climbs and scenic, smoother paths.

Q: Is parking available near the gravel bike trailheads in Olot?
A: Yes, Olot, being a popular base for outdoor activities, offers various parking options. Many trailheads, especially those connected to the Via Verde del Carrilet or within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking.

Q: How can I reach the gravel bike trails in Olot using public transport?
A: Olot is accessible by bus services connecting it to larger cities like Girona and Barcelona. Once in Olot, many gravel routes, including sections of the Via Verde del Carrilet, are easily reachable from the town center, making it convenient to start your ride without a car.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the gravel bike routes?
A: Yes, as you cycle through the Garrotxa region, you'll find charming medieval towns and villages like Santa Pau and Castellfollit de la Roca. These often have local cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and enjoy regional cuisine. Olot itself also offers numerous options before and after your ride.

Q: What makes the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park special for gravel biking?
A: The Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park is unique due to its approximately 40 volcanic cones and over 20 lava flows, creating a distinctive and rolling landscape. This geological heritage, combined with lush forests and well-maintained greenways, provides an unparalleled backdrop for gravel biking, offering both scenic beauty and varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time to visit for gravel biking in Olot?
A: The Garrotxa region offers pleasant conditions for gravel biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially when the leaves change color in the Fageda d'en Jordà. Summers can be warm, but many routes offer shaded sections through forests. Winters are generally mild, making year-round cycling possible.

Q: Are the gravel bike trails in Olot dog-friendly?
A: Many natural areas and trails in the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. However, specific regulations may apply in certain protected areas or on private land. It's always best to check local signage or park guidelines for the specific route you plan to take.
tips: Near a small waterfall on the Fluvià river in Sant Joan les Fonts, a panoramic point allows you to observe something truly unique: the three so-called Molì, Fondo and Boscarrò lava flows one on top of the other. They appeared in the sunlight thanks to the erosion carried out over the centuries by the waters of the Rio Fluvià, whose course favored the settlement and birth of various villages in these areas.

The small village of Castellfollit de la Roca stands on a spectacular basaltic cliff, 50 meters high and almost a kilometer long, formed by the flow of the Fluvià river. The oldest part of the village dates back to the Middle Ages and is made up of squares, streets and shaded corners that offer relief from the summer heat. The narrow alleys of the village lead to the old church of Sant Salvador which dates back to the 13th century. From here you can enjoy an unparalleled panorama of the lush valley below: Castellfollit is in fact located close to the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Nature Reserve.

This long path between Besalù and Santa Pau is a true off-road paradise: without traffic and entirely in the woods, it has a very simple gravel surface, which is easily passable even by gravel bicycles.

waypoints.name: View of the Molí, Fondo, and Boscarró Lava Flows
Gravel Road Between Besalú and Santa Pau
